news.title: Moody's cuts rating on private credit fund run by KKR and Future Standard to junk as bad loans grow
news.summary: Moody's is the latest sign of distress in private credit. Retail investors have been rushing to withdraw funds from Blackstone to Apollo.

news.title: Oil rises with Brent climbing back above $100 as optimism fades over Iran war de-escalation
news.summary: Brent crude climbs back above $100 per barrel. Oil prices traded higher on Tuesday, with international benchmark Brent crude climbing backabove $100.

news.title: United ditches more economy seats to make room for bigger premium cabins with new layouts
news.summary: United will have new cabins on smaller planes with premium seats, including those that convert into a bed. United wants to win more sales from high-spending travelers.

news.title: Property Play: Home flippers see smallest profits since the Great Recession, real estate data firm says
news.summary: The typical home flip netted investors just $65,981 in gross profit last year. That's a 25.5% return on investment.

news.title: EV battery startup pivots to defense industry amid Iran war, weak electric vehicle market
news.summary: Former GM executive CEO Pamela Fletcher told CNBC "the faster path, and frankly, a big need, is out there in this defense space"

news.title: Flights will keep getting more expensive and harder to find. Here’s how much worse it could get.
news.summary: Travelers are struggling to score deals on airfares during an already chaotic travel season. Airlines cut flight routes and raise prices to combat rising jet-fuel prices.

news.title: As markets wobble on Iran war worries, one big bank is raising its S&P 500 target
news.summary: Barclays: A sturdy U.S. economy and AI-related activity will boost corporate earnings. Barclays: "AI-related activities will boost earnings for companies across the globe"

news.title: Smart ways to save money on gas right now
news.summary: You won't believe how expensive World Cup tickets are for this summer’s games. Plus: You won’t believe how much it costs to buy a ticket to a World Cup game.

news.title: It’s like the sun exploding: One Wall Street firm fears $200 oil — and says it’s not too late for investors to prepare
news.summary: Citi sees  oil prices reaching as high as $200 in a scenario where disruptions continue through the end of June. Citi: Oil prices could reach $200 a barrel if disruptions continue.

news.title: Wall Street bears may have pushed the stock market too far, making an April rally more likely
news.summary: Citadel Securities’ Scott Rubner sees an “increasingly constructive” setup for April when it comes to stocks. Rubner: “I think we’re going to see an increasing number of positive catalysts in the coming weeks.”
